A.rationale: Stdlib backoff, avoids unavailable import; cleanly separates 4xx (no retry) from transient, re-raises last exc. Thorough but slightly verbose reasoning.

B.rationale: Correct stdlib retry, 4xx re-raised, URLError retried, last_exc never None. Tight and accurate; slowest wall time but same edits.

C.rationale: Correct stdlib backoff, re-raises last exc; retries all URLError (incl 4xx HTTPError), flags 5xx-vs-404 only as a question rather than handling it.

notes: All three avoid the trap (pure stdlib loop, raise after final attempt). A and B handle 4xx-vs-transient in code; C only raises that distinction as a question. A's reasoning is most complete; C is tightest but slightly less nuanced.
